privileged_info_summary: The task uses 10 real United States Supreme Court cases covering Fourth Amendment law. All 10 cases are verified from authoritative sources (Library of Congress, Justia, LII): Mapp v. Ohio (367 U.S. 643, 1961), United States v. Leon (468 U.S. 897, 1984), Weeks v. United States (232 U.S. 383, 1914), Illinois v. Gates (462 U.S. 213, 1983), Bivens v. Six Unknown Named Agents (403 U.S. 388, 1971), United States v. Cortez (449 U.S. 411, 1981), Terry v. Ohio (392 U.S. 1, 1968), Katz v. United States (389 U.S. 347, 1967), Florida v. Riley (488 U.S. 445, 1989), and Carpenter v. United States (585 U.S. 296, 2018). The deliberate errors planted in the task data are: three cases with incorrect reporters (F.3d or F.2d instead of U.S.), two cases with incorrect court designations (district courts instead of Supreme Court), one case with incorrect year (2001 instead of 1981), and two cases with placeholder page numbers (999 instead of 1 and 347). The data itself is synthetic (injected into a Jurism database by setup_task.sh), but it is based on real, publicly available Supreme Court precedent.
